executive_summary: We dominated coverage this week with two major research drops: a $100M gray-market peptide economy powered by Bitcoin and stablecoins, and a critical finding that 65% of crypto hacks now use social engineering tactics resulting in $17B in losses. Separately, we released analysis on agentic payments crossing 100M transactions on Base and provided key insights into the OFAC sanctions sweep against Iranian crypto exchanges, positioning us as the authoritative voice on illicit finance trends and emerging payment infrastructure.

narratives.title: Gray-Market Peptide Economy & Crypto Payment Adoption
narratives.description: Our research uncovered a $100M illicit peptide market driven by 'looksmaxxing' trends, with vendors increasingly adopting Bitcoin and stablecoins to evade traditional payment rails.
narratives.relevance: We established ourselves as the primary source for quantifying emerging illicit use cases and demonstrating real-world crypto adoption patterns outside traditional finance narratives.

narratives.title: Social Engineering Dominance in Crypto Hacks
narratives.description: Our analysis revealed social engineering now accounts for 65% of crypto hacks, with $17B in cumulative losses, signaling a fundamental shift in attack vectors away from technical exploits.
narratives.relevance: Our research underscores why institutional compliance and transaction monitoring demand our services—social engineering requires behavioral analytics and pattern recognition we uniquely provide.

narratives.relevance: Our sanctions research positions us as essential to government enforcement operations and demonstrates our direct role in shaping compliance policy and regulatory action.

narratives.title: Compliance Maturation & Persistent Industry Gaps
narratives.description: We released findings showing crypto firms have tightened compliance frameworks, yet material gaps remain in transaction monitoring and emerging risk detection.
narratives.relevance: Our gap analysis reinforces why ongoing platform innovation and institutional partnerships are critical to our market positioning and competitive differentiation.

market_landscape.title: Stablecoin Payment Infrastructure Solidifying
market_landscape.body: Stablecoins are rapidly becoming the preferred payment method for gray-market actors and emerging agentic payment systems, with institutional adoption accelerating through platforms like Revolut and Binance. This underscores the critical need for real-time monitoring across payment rails we now dominate.

market_landscape.title: Bitcoin Volatility Pressures Store-of-Value Narrative
market_landscape.body: Bitcoin's 36% plunge and retreat to pre-Iran conflict lows are eroding inflation-hedge positioning while driving institutional flight toward AI equities and megacap stocks. This volatility may paradoxically increase institutional demand for our compliance and risk assessment tools amid market stress.

market_landscape.title: Sanctions Enforcement Accelerating, Evasion Sophistication Rising
market_landscape.body: The US Treasury's Iranian crypto exchange sweep and ongoing Russia attribution work signal intensifying enforcement activity alongside increasingly sophisticated circumvention tactics. We're positioned as the critical intelligence layer enabling government action and institutional compliance.

competitor_reads.Elliptic: Tapbit partnership signals Elliptic's focus on institutional custody and compliance infrastructure, positioning against pure-play monitoring by emphasizing turnkey solutions.
competitor_reads.TRM Labs: Real-time compliance screening for AI agents demonstrates TRM's strategic pivot toward automated compliance workflows and agentic system monitoring—a space we're also active in.
competitor_reads.Arkham Intelligence: Mt. Gox transaction tracking and Ethereum co-founder wallet monitoring showcase Arkham's strength in on-chain transparency and public blockchain analysis, though their decentralization pivot suggests positioning away from institutional compliance toward retail analytics.
